description_long: This house features a traditional timber-framed design with a prominent dark gable roof and cream-colored infill panels. The front facade includes a central entryway flanked by rectangular windows, with one window slightly ajar, suggesting functional shutters or casements. The side and rear elevations display a grid of exposed wooden beams, characteristic of half-timbered architecture, and minimal window openings. The roof is steeply pitched and constructed with evenly spaced, dark shingles, giving the structure a robust and weather-resistant appearance. The overall form is rectangular with a symmetrical gable on both ends, and the house sits on a slightly raised base. The materials and style evoke a classic European or Tudor cottage, suitable for residential neighborhoods or historical urban settings.
description: A timber-framed house with a steep dark gable roof, cream infill panels, and a central entryway flanked by windows. The design is symmetrical and evokes a classic European cottage style.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entryway, two windows (one open), and the timber-framed facade.
description_view_1: Side gable view with a grid of exposed beams and a single small window near the base.
description_view_2: Rear view with a continuous row of cream panels and no visible windows or doors.
description_view_3: Opposite gable end with symmetrical timber framing and no windows.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical placement on front, minimal on sides and rear
attribute_entryway_design: Central door with rectangular windows, no porch or overhang, simple timber frame detailing
